sourceEvidence.label: Rotator Cuff Tear — Learning objectives
sourceEvidence.summary: Static Prepare Learning objectives for Rotator Cuff Tear

objectives: Recognize common cuff tear presentations and key exam findings.
Explain the role of x-rays and MRI in workup.
Outline initial conservative care and when surgery becomes more urgent.

sourceEvidence.label: Rotator Cuff Tear — Fast study template
sourceEvidence.summary: Static Prepare Fast study template for Rotator Cuff Tear

mustKnow: Night pain, weakness, active versus passive ROM, traumatic acute tears
payload.oneLiner: Rotator cuff tears are shoulder dysfunction problems where age, acuity, and functional loss shape urgency.
pimpQuestions: Why are acute traumatic tears different?
What x-ray findings suggest chronic cuff disease?

sourceEvidence.label: Rotator Cuff Tear — Deep study template
sourceEvidence.summary: Static Prepare Deep study template for Rotator Cuff Tear

imaging: MRI for tendon quality, retraction, and atrophy
decisionMaking: Symptoms, function, tear size, chronicity, patient goals
boardPearls: Passive ROM preserved with weak active elevation suggests cuff pathology rather than adhesive capsulitis
